name: Roseanne Barr
bio: Roseanne Barr is an American comedian, actress, and writer who rose from the stand-up circuit with a working-class, self-styled domestic goddess persona that challenged the conventions of housewife comedy. Her breakout appearance on The Tonight Show in 1985 led to the ABC sitcom Roseanne, on which she played the lead and won an Emmy and a Golden Globe. Her later career included political commentary and public controversies, including the 2018 cancellation of the Roseanne revival after a racist tweet.

timeline.ev: Breakout stand-up set on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson
Stars in HBO special The Roseanne Barr Show
Roseanne revival airs and is cancelled after a controversial tweet

accolades: Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Lead Actress in a Comedy Series (1993)
Golden Globe Award for Best Actress in a Television Series Musical or Comedy
